page: Microsoft Azure DC32as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCASv5 server family. Powered by an x86_64 AMD processor, this instance features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. The underlying virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation for consistent compute performance.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all operating system and application data must be hosted on external volumes. It is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance persistent disk options to meet specific input/output requirements. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external network configuration or network address translation for public internet connectivity.

From a resource density perspective, the DC32as_v5 offers a balanced hardware profile suitable for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without the overhead of local storage. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a viable option for general-purpose applications, enterprise application hosting, medium-sized databases, and development environments that rely on external persistent storage solutions.
description: Microsoft Azure DC32as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DCASv5 family, powered by an x86_64 AMD processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. Virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance guarantees dedicated CPU allocation for stable performance. The server does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach high-performance external block storage. It also lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses, necessitating external networking configurations. With its balanced resource allocation, the DC32as_v5 is designed for general-purpose workloads, enterprise application hosting, and database deployments that utilize external storage.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
